title: ONiLAB Magnetic Stirrer with 4PCS Stir Bars，Magnetic Mixer with Stirring Capacity 3000ml (No Heating)
description:
Compact Design and Easy Use: Compact design with small footprint for Portable use, 12 Voltage available for mobile use, lightweight and quiet operation. Not suitable for highly viscous solutions
High Lab Quality Material:Maintenance free brushless DC motor enable stable and strength operation with stepless speed and long lifespan
Powerful Functions: Max stirring quantity 3L; Default PET top provides excellent chemical resistance and easy cleaning
Wide Use: Perfect for stirring in scientific research, industry, agriculture, health and medicine, homebrew, like brew yeast starters, wine making, Juice mixing, wine decanting and etc;
1 Year Warranty: we are manufacturer which has more than 20 years experience in the field of lab products. . Any quality problem, we will provide free replacement
reviews_5s:
I took a chance on this, although I did not know if it would work for my purpose.

I am using this to mix small batch peach and pear wine must in one gallon fermenting jars with 4" lids during the primary fermentation, rather than risk contamination from using a stirring rod.

Even though the jars have a relatively thick curved upward bottom, are considerably larger than the stirrer, and are in a plastic storage box, the magnets are strong enough that I am able to run about 60% speed. Fast enough to oxygenate and mix well.

Using the magnetic stirrer four time a day for 2 minutes sped up the aerobic growth stage of the fermenting cycle to half the time compared to using a stirring rod. Once the yeast bubbling was strong enough to keep the must mixed, I put an airlocks on and stopped stirring. This batch is going to be finished in record time.

Two notes: it would be more convienient if I could put rechargable batteries in it, but since it runs on 12VDC, I just plugged it into a small UB1280 SLA battery I use for other things. Given the price, this works for me. The charger cord is too short to use on the kitchen table.

Second, the magnets have a raised ring around the middle. (Type "B"?) I think this helps a lot on the curved bottom of the jars.

I like this so much, I ordered a second one so I can leave both under a pair of jars in a plastic tote for the three days I need it. That will save time, and make ferment startup much more convienient. The plastic tote makes the whole setup very stable even though the jar bottom is curved and bigger than the stirrer.I am a retired chemist. I missed having a magnetic stirrer so I got this one for use in the home. It helps me stir mixtures in the kitchen. lol, you can take the scientist out of the lab but not the lab ot of the scientist. This stirrer works well. I would like to have a heater/stirrer in one unit but for the price this was a good device even though it doesnt heat. It comes with different size magnets, but the only one of use is the large one. I will order larger magnets. The unit is not chemical resistant so I would not mix strong acids/bases with this: For that you need a ceramic top.First time buyer. Seems to be very well constructed. Works wellVery glad I kept looking & got this one instead of cheaper smaller models. I needed a very gentle stir with stable stance. Fits a half gallon mason jar easily. The lowest setting stir is steady even though jar is not perfectly flat at base. Control knob allows for faster stir if desired. Worth the extra $ for very good quality.I got it to replace another brand of mixer bought here on Amz, but found it a bit too small for my use. It did work well, although I didn't care for the slow start feature. Many users prefer this, but not meThis little fella works great for the price! Comes with 4 stirring magnets and spins great in quart Mason jars. I'm just using it for water basically. I have no idea how it does with thicker liquid.Buenas calidad y rápida entregadid a good job in my darkroomThis is for the slightly more costly square model. Good stirrer, though like all of the mag stirrers of this range/consumer class, the speed control isn't great, and the magnet strength is medium. But good enough. The speed control starts off at 0 at the on position, and to get it spinning you need to increase to a medium speed, then drop it back down if you want it slower so as to not cause a vortex. I'm used to Corning and other research grade instruments, but I'll let this pass at 1/10 the cost. But it's actually a lot better control than the Amazon recommended Intllab. I just returned that one. And with the square you get an assortment of stir bars, which is nice since I didn't have some of the smaller sizes, and the magnetic retriever is very useful. Good deal.Perfect stirplate for homeschool mycology . We use this for liquid culture and it seems to work great for quart size jars and smaller . I haven't tried anything larger than quart size jars .
reviews_1s:
I'm using the stir plate for a beer yeast starter. In a 3 liter flask, with 1 L of wort, the stir rod doesn't ever catch on the magnetic. It just flies off, like there isn't end attraction to hold it. I tried the included 1" rod, to no avail. Switching to a 2 L flask with the 1" rod. I get almost no vortex . Tried a 2" bar and it flew off every time. It took 20 minutes to get the damn 1" rod to even catch, ridiculous. I'm returning it as it is totally unusable.Opened box, powered up, nothing happens. Feels cheaply made, but I haven’t used others to make a fair comparison. Disappointed.Product stopped working after a single use.It’s very quiet, material seems very solid. Very easy to clean, it definitely seems as big as you’d expect, but the weakness of the magnet that holds the stirrer in place is not good at all. Any speed higher than very low caused the stirrer to just fly off the magnet and stop spinning. Big let down.Play Video
Replacement requested. Will update review after I see how the replacement performs.

Update: same thing happened with replacement. I think they must use a really weak/low quality motor in these things.Purchased it to stir my C-60 mixture and it is not very good at it, cost me a lot of money in ingredients for nothing ; since I couldnt accomplish the taskWorked as advertised first two times, on the third the power supply quit.only lasted a few weeks. lessoned learned an extra 10 bucks will be worth it.It just vibrated ever so lightly. I waste of my money!It is crap and died within days of use. tossed it in the garbage.
